question.text: Which of the following statements about Gross Total Income (GTI) and Total Income (TI) is correct?

answer.text: Total Income is always greater than or equal to Gross Total Income
Gross Total Income is computed after deductions under Chapter VI-A
Gross Total Income is the aggregate of income under all five heads after clubbing provisions and set-off of losses, while Total Income is GTI minus deductions under Chapter VI-A
Total Income and Gross Total Income are the same if the assessee claims deductions under Section 80C

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per Section 80B(5), Gross Total Income (GTI) is computed by: (1) computing income under each of the five heads (Salary, House Property, Business/Profession, Capital Gains, Other Sources), (2) applying clubbing provisions, and (3) setting off losses. Total Income = GTI minus deductions under Chapter VI-A (Sections 80C to 80U). Thus, TI ≤ GTI always. **The Trap:** Option (a) reverses the relationship — TI cannot exceed GTI since deductions reduce income. Option (b) gets the sequence wrong — GTI is computed before Chapter VI-A deductions. Option (d) contradicts itself — if 80C deductions are claimed, TI would be less than GTI, not the same. **Cross-Exam Context:** This distinction is fundamental and tested across NISM X-A and X-B certification exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always compute GTI first before advising on deductions — some deductions have limits linked to GTI, and certain deductions are not available if GTI is below certain thresholds.
